content.content:
1	==== Decision
2	
3	IAM propagation delays for Cloud Build v2 connection creation are handled by
4	connection-creation retry with exponential backoff, not by an impersonation
5	readiness gate or fixed sleep delays.
6	
7	* **SA binding propagation**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy` calls that fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
8	  (newly-created service account not yet visible to the IAM policy service) are retried with exponential backoff.
9	* **Secret access readiness**: The CB v2 connection creation LRO itself serves as the propagation test.
10	  If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (IAM grant not propagated), the LRO fails.
11	  The caller retries with exponential backoff, deleting the zombie connection resource between attempts.
12	* **Secret resource propagation**: `getIamPolicy` on a newly created secret may return HTTP 404 while
13	  the Secret Manager resource propagates across Google's control plane.  The API documents that
14	  `getIamPolicy` returns an empty policy (HTTP 200) when no policy has been set — so 404 indicates
15	  the resource itself is not yet visible, not that the policy is absent.  After creating secrets,
16	  `rbgu_poll_until_ok` polls each secret's `getIamPolicy` endpoint until 200 before proceeding to
17	  grant IAM bindings.
18	
19	==== Rationale
20	
21	The connection creation LRO tests the actual operation, not a proxy.
22	When the Cloud Build Service Agent accesses resources requiring recently-granted permissions,
23	a successful LRO proves end-to-end readiness: the IAM grant has propagated,
24	the service agent identity is active, and the resource content is valid.
25	
26	Fixed `sleep` delays ("sleep and pray") are unreliable:
27	
28	* Google's IAM propagation documentation states changes "typically take less than 2 minutes"
29	  but provides no guaranteed upper bound.
30	* A 15-second sleep is too short for worst-case propagation and wastefully long for the common case.
31	* Retry-on-error tests the actual operation: if the grant has propagated, the retry succeeds immediately;
32	  if not, the retry continues until the deadline.
33	
34	The `testIamPermissions` API cannot serve as a readiness gate because it only tests the caller's own
35	permissions on a resource -- it cannot test whether a third-party service account (like the CB service agent)
36	has been granted access to a secret.
37	
38	==== Superseded: Impersonation Readiness Gate
39	
40	The prior approach used an impersonation readiness gate: mint a short-lived token as the CB service agent
41	via `generateAccessToken`, then call `accessSecretVersion` with that impersonated token, retrying on 403
42	until the grant propagates.  This approach was abandoned due to three problems discovered 2026-03-03:
43	
44	. **roles/owner does NOT grant iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken** --
45	  The explicit `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant (previously deleted as unnecessary per
46	  an earlier RBSCIP revision) was in fact required for impersonation.  Without it,
47	  `generateAccessToken` returns persistent 403.  roles/owner is a broad role but does not include
48	  the `iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken` permission needed for impersonation.
49	
50	. **Wrong SA identity** -- The code impersonated the default build SA
51	  (`{PN}@cloudbuild.gserviceaccount.com`) but CB v2 connections use the Cloud Build Service Agent
52	  (`service-{PN}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).  Even if impersonation worked,
53	  it would have tested the wrong principal.
54	
55	. **No non-impersonation alternative exists** -- `testIamPermissions` and IAM Policy Analyzer
56	  reflect policy intent (what the policy document says), not enforcement state (whether the grant
57	  has actually propagated).  Only actual access as the principal proves propagation.
58	
59	Pattern B (connection-creation retry) eliminates `generateAccessToken`, `accessSecretVersion`,
60	and any need for `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grants for the readiness gate.
61	
62	==== Evidence
63	
64	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
65	|===
66	|Claim
67	|Source
68	
69	|IAM changes propagate eventually, typically < 2 minutes
70	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/access-change-propagation[Access change propagation] -- "After you grant access, the access change takes effect after a period of time known as propagation delay"
71	
72	|Newly created SA may not be immediately usable
73	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] -- "After you create a service account, you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account"
74	
75	|testIamPermissions tests caller permissions only
76	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/testing-permissions[Testing permissions] -- tests whether the caller has the specified permissions on a resource
77	
78	|CB v2 connection creation uses the Cloud Build Service Agent to read secrets
79	|https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/automating-builds/github/connect-repo-github[Connect a GitHub host] -- the Cloud Build Service Agent must have `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` on the secrets used by the connection
80	|===
81	
82	==== What Dies
83	
84	* The impersonation readiness gate (`generateAccessToken` + `accessSecretVersion` as a pre-check)
85	* Any `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant solely for the readiness gate
86	  (build-time Mason impersonation grants are unrelated and preserved)
87	* Fixed `sleep 15` delays before CB v2 connection creation and after Governor SA creation
88	* Standalone `rbgu_poll_until_ok` as a correctness gate for IAM propagation
89	  (the SA GET poll remains as a fast-path existence check, not a grant propagation gate)
90	* Any assumption that SA existence implies SA usability in IAM policy operations
91	
92	==== What Survives
93	
94	* SA GET poll via `rbgu_poll_until_ok` -- retained as a fast-path check that the SA resource itself
95	  is readable, which typically resolves well before IAM policy propagation completes
96	* The existing 429/5xx transient retry loop in {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} step 3 --
97	  this handles a different failure class (rate limiting, server errors) and is preserved inside
98	  the new outer propagation retry
99	* All IAM grant functions ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo},
100	  {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}) -- enhanced with propagation retry,
101	  not replaced
102	* Cloud Build service agent `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant on Mason SA --
103	  this is for build-time impersonation (Cloud Build executing as Mason), unrelated to the readiness gate
104	
105	==== Backoff Profile
106	
107	All propagation retries use the same profile:
108	
109	* Initial delay: 3 seconds
110	* Multiplier: 2x
111	* Cap: 20 seconds
112	* Deadline: 420 seconds (7 minutes)
113	
114	This profile accommodates Google's documented "typically < 2 minutes" with margin for worst-case
115	propagation while avoiding indefinite waits.
116	
117	==== References
118	
119	* Research conducted: 2026-03-03 (API documentation review, live 403 diagnosis)
120	* Google IAM documentation consistently describes eventual consistency without hard SLA bounds
121	* Pattern B (connection-creation-as-probe) replaces the original `generateAccessToken` / `accessSecretVersion` gate
122

file.content:
==== Decision

IAM propagation delays for Cloud Build v2 connection creation are handled by
connection-creation retry with exponential backoff, not by an impersonation
readiness gate or fixed sleep delays.

* **SA binding propagation**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy` calls that fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
  (newly-created service account not yet visible to the IAM policy service) are retried with exponential backoff.
* **Secret access readiness**: The CB v2 connection creation LRO itself serves as the propagation test.
  If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (IAM grant not propagated), the LRO fails.
  The caller retries with exponential backoff, deleting the zombie connection resource between attempts.
* **Secret resource propagation**: `getIamPolicy` on a newly created secret may return HTTP 404 while
  the Secret Manager resource propagates across Google's control plane.  The API documents that
  `getIamPolicy` returns an empty policy (HTTP 200) when no policy has been set — so 404 indicates
  the resource itself is not yet visible, not that the policy is absent.  After creating secrets,
  `rbgu_poll_until_ok` polls each secret's `getIamPolicy` endpoint until 200 before proceeding to
  grant IAM bindings.

==== Rationale

The connection creation LRO tests the actual operation, not a proxy.
When the Cloud Build Service Agent accesses resources requiring recently-granted permissions,
a successful LRO proves end-to-end readiness: the IAM grant has propagated,
the service agent identity is active, and the resource content is valid.

Fixed `sleep` delays ("sleep and pray") are unreliable:

* Google's IAM propagation documentation states changes "typically take less than 2 minutes"
  but provides no guaranteed upper bound.
* A 15-second sleep is too short for worst-case propagation and wastefully long for the common case.
* Retry-on-error tests the actual operation: if the grant has propagated, the retry succeeds immediately;
  if not, the retry continues until the deadline.

The `testIamPermissions` API cannot serve as a readiness gate because it only tests the caller's own
permissions on a resource -- it cannot test whether a third-party service account (like the CB service agent)
has been granted access to a secret.

==== Superseded: Impersonation Readiness Gate

The prior approach used an impersonation readiness gate: mint a short-lived token as the CB service agent
via `generateAccessToken`, then call `accessSecretVersion` with that impersonated token, retrying on 403
until the grant propagates.  This approach was abandoned due to three problems discovered 2026-03-03:

. **roles/owner does NOT grant iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken** --
  The explicit `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant (previously deleted as unnecessary per
  an earlier RBSCIP revision) was in fact required for impersonation.  Without it,
  `generateAccessToken` returns persistent 403.  roles/owner is a broad role but does not include
  the `iam.serviceAccounts.getAccessToken` permission needed for impersonation.

. **Wrong SA identity** -- The code impersonated the default build SA
  (`{PN}@cloudbuild.gserviceaccount.com`) but CB v2 connections use the Cloud Build Service Agent
  (`service-{PN}@gcp-sa-cloudbuild.iam.gserviceaccount.com`).  Even if impersonation worked,
  it would have tested the wrong principal.

. **No non-impersonation alternative exists** -- `testIamPermissions` and IAM Policy Analyzer
  reflect policy intent (what the policy document says), not enforcement state (whether the grant
  has actually propagated).  Only actual access as the principal proves propagation.

Pattern B (connection-creation retry) eliminates `generateAccessToken`, `accessSecretVersion`,
and any need for `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grants for the readiness gate.

==== Evidence

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Claim
|Source

|IAM changes propagate eventually, typically < 2 minutes
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/access-change-propagation[Access change propagation] -- "After you grant access, the access change takes effect after a period of time known as propagation delay"

|Newly created SA may not be immediately usable
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] -- "After you create a service account, you might need to wait 60 seconds or more before you use the service account"

|testIamPermissions tests caller permissions only
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/testing-permissions[Testing permissions] -- tests whether the caller has the specified permissions on a resource

|CB v2 connection creation uses the Cloud Build Service Agent to read secrets
|https://cloud.google.com/build/docs/automating-builds/github/connect-repo-github[Connect a GitHub host] -- the Cloud Build Service Agent must have `roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor` on the secrets used by the connection
|===

==== What Dies

* The impersonation readiness gate (`generateAccessToken` + `accessSecretVersion` as a pre-check)
* Any `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant solely for the readiness gate
  (build-time Mason impersonation grants are unrelated and preserved)
* Fixed `sleep 15` delays before CB v2 connection creation and after Governor SA creation
* Standalone `rbgu_poll_until_ok` as a correctness gate for IAM propagation
  (the SA GET poll remains as a fast-path existence check, not a grant propagation gate)
* Any assumption that SA existence implies SA usability in IAM policy operations

==== What Survives

* SA GET poll via `rbgu_poll_until_ok` -- retained as a fast-path check that the SA resource itself
  is readable, which typically resolves well before IAM policy propagation completes
* The existing 429/5xx transient retry loop in {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} step 3 --
  this handles a different failure class (rate limiting, server errors) and is preserved inside
  the new outer propagation retry
* All IAM grant functions ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_repo},
  {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}) -- enhanced with propagation retry,
  not replaced
* Cloud Build service agent `roles/iam.serviceAccountTokenCreator` grant on Mason SA --
  this is for build-time impersonation (Cloud Build executing as Mason), unrelated to the readiness gate

==== Backoff Profile

All propagation retries use the same profile:

* Initial delay: 3 seconds
* Multiplier: 2x
* Cap: 20 seconds
* Deadline: 420 seconds (7 minutes)

This profile accommodates Google's documented "typically < 2 minutes" with margin for worst-case
propagation while avoiding indefinite waits.

==== References

* Research conducted: 2026-03-03 (API documentation review, live 403 diagnosis)
* Google IAM documentation consistently describes eventual consistency without hard SLA bounds
* Pattern B (connection-creation-as-probe) replaces the original `generateAccessToken` / `accessSecretVersion` gate

content.content:
200	version=1 does not downgrade a version=3 policy when the mask is defaulted —
201	the version field is simply ignored.
202	
203	===== Empty-Policy / No-Policy Behavior
204	
205	[cols="2,3,1",options="header"]
206	|===
207	|API
208	|getIamPolicy on new resource
209	|404 possible?
210	
211	|CRM Projects ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project})
212	|200 + policy with bindings (project born with Owner grant)
213	|No (403 if project does not exist — deliberate enumeration prevention)
214	
215	|IAM {giam_service_account_s} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_sa})
216	|200 + empty policy with etag
217	|No
218	
219	|{gar_registry} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo})
220	|200 + empty policy with etag
221	|No
222	
223	|Secret Manager
224	|200 + empty policy with etag
225	|No (404 = secret itself does not exist)
226	
227	|{gcs_service} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket})
228	|200 + policy with inherited bindings
229	|No
230	|===
231	
232	**No API returns 404 for "no IAM policy."**
233	All resources that accept allow policies are created with default allow policies.
234	`getIamPolicy` returns 200 with an empty-ish policy (no bindings, but etag and
235	version present) for resources that have never had explicit IAM configuration.
236	
237	===== Propagation Error Strings
238	
239	The HTTP 400 errors containing `"does not exist"` and `"is not deleted"` that
240	the grant functions retry on (per <<rbscip_trade_study>> backoff profile) are
241	**empirically stable but not contractually guaranteed**.
242	Google does not document error message text as stable API surface.
243	The HTTP status code (400) is the stable signal; the message content is
244	diagnostic, not machine-parseable contract.
245	
246	The two patterns represent different propagation directions:
247	
248	* `"does not exist"` — forward propagation: a newly-created {giam_service_account}
249	  is not yet visible to the IAM policy service as a valid member.  The SA
250	  resource has been created (GET returns 200) but the policy service has not yet
251	  learned about it.
252	
253	* `"is not deleted"` — backward propagation: a recently-deleted
254	  {giam_service_account} is still referenced in existing policy bindings.
255	  The SA resource has been deleted but the policy service still considers it
256	  a valid (or at least referenced) member.
257	
258	Multiple independent ecosystem sources (Terraform provider, Red Hat OpenShift
259	installer, Pulumi) have depended on these strings for years without breakage.
260	The Terraform Google provider explicitly treats HTTP 400 as retryable in IAM
261	contexts (issue #8280), which constitutes strong ecosystem evidence that this
262	pattern is reliable enough for major infrastructure tools.
263	The practical risk of string instability is low but nonzero.
264	
265	**SA email vs numeric ID propagation.**
266	Google's documentation recommends using a service account's numeric ID rather
267	than its email address to reference it immediately after creation: "To identify
268	a service account just after it is created, use its numeric ID rather than its
269	email address."  Recipe Bottle uses email addresses throughout, which is why
270	propagation retry is necessary.  Switching to numeric ID could reduce (but
271	likely not eliminate) propagation delays.
272	
273	**SA keys.create write-path propagation.**
274	Observed 2026-03-12 during fresh depot onboarding: `serviceAccounts.keys.create`
275	POST returned HTTP 404 "Service account ... does not exist" immediately after
276	both `serviceAccounts.get` (GET by email) and `serviceAccounts.keys.list` (GET)
277	succeeded on the same SA email path.  This demonstrates a third propagation
278	class: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) propagate independently
279	from SA write operations (keys.create) on the same endpoint family.
280	The 404 response confirms the POST was rejected without side effects — no key
281	was created — making retry safe.  `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list`
282	confirmed zero USER_MANAGED keys after the 404 failure.
283	The race is intermittent: a subsequent director creation (dir1011) with the same
284	code path succeeded on first attempt.
285	Mitigation: retry keys.create POST on 404 up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX`
286	times with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts.
287	
288	===== Secret Manager Regional Endpoints
289	
290	Secret Manager supports both global and regional secret endpoints:
291	
292	* Global: `projects/*/secrets/*`
293	* Regional: `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*`
294	
295	The service proto defines `additional_bindings` for regional secrets at both
296	`getIamPolicy` and `setIamPolicy`.  The current {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret} grants use global endpoints.  If Recipe Bottle ever uses regional
297	secrets, the IAM grant patterns would need to construct regional resource paths.
298	Noting for completeness.
299	
300	===== Current Code vs Actual API Contract
301	
302	This section catalogs discrepancies between the current implementation and the
303	verified API contracts.  These are not necessarily bugs that cause failures —
304	several work by accident or are protected by the single-writer operational
305	invariant — but they represent incorrect assumptions in the code.
306	
307	[cols="1,2,3",options="header"]
308	|===
309	|Pattern
310	|Discrepancy
311	|Detail
312	
313	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_project}
314	|412 handler targets wrong error code
315	|Code handles 412 as "etag mismatch" (line 199 of rbgi_IAM.sh).
316	 CRM actually returns 409 ABORTED.
317	 The 412 case never fires; etag mismatches hit the 409 path
318	 (`buc_die "HTTP 409 Conflict (fatal by invariant)"`), which
319	 produces the correct outcome (fatal) but with the wrong diagnostic.
320	
321	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
322	|404 handler for impossible response
323	|Code initializes vanilla policy on HTTP 404 (line 304).
324	 {gar_registry} `getIamPolicy` returns 200 with empty policy
325	 for existing repos, never 404.
326	 A 404 would mean the repository itself does not exist — vanilla init
327	 would mask this real error.
328	
329	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
330	|Resolved — uses GET per proto
331	|The shared `rbgi_add_repo_iam_role` function already used GET.
332	 The inline director AR grant path in `rbgg_Governor.sh` was the
333	 remaining POST site — observed failing with HTTP 404 on 2026-03-12
334	 (transcoding layer rejecting POST on GET-only endpoint).  Fixed to
335	 GET with `requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter.  The 404
336	 vanilla-init handler was also removed (404 on GET means repo does
337	 not exist — a real error, not a policy absence).
338	
339	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
340	|Skips available etag
341	|Passes empty string for etag (line 315), performing unconditional
342	 overwrite despite the API always returning etag.
343	 Safe under single-writer invariant but discards protection the API
344	 provides for free.  See "Accidental etag passthrough" above — the
345	 actual behavior may be passthrough rather than omission, but this
346	 is accidental, not intentional.
347	
348	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}
349	|Vanilla init for impossible response
350	|Code initializes vanilla policy on non-200 (line 411).
351	 IAM `getIamPolicy` returns 200 with empty policy for existing SAs.
352	 Non-200 means the SA does not exist or access denied — vanilla init
353	 masks a real error.
354	
355	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}
356	|Skips available etag
357	|Passes empty string for etag (line 419), performing unconditional
358	 overwrite.  Same single-writer safety, same accidental passthrough
359	 possibility, same missed intentional protection.
360	
361	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
362	|Etag described as conditional
363	|Code extracts etag with fallback to empty (line 507).
364	 {gcs_service} `getIamPolicy` always returns etag — the fallback
365	 should never trigger.  Minor: the RBS0 spec previously said "when
366	 present" but it is always present.
367	
368	|Secret Manager ({rbtoe_iam_grant_secret})
369	|Resolved — now has propagation retry
370	|The three secret IAM grants (formerly inline in {rbtgo_depot_levy})
371	 are now extracted into {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret} with full propagation
372	 retry per <<rbscip_trade_study>> backoff profile, explicit etag,
373	 transient retry, and `requestedPolicyVersion=3`.
374	
375	|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
376	|No requestedPolicyVersion in getIamPolicy
377	|Only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} requests policy version 3.
378	 The other three do not, creating a latent hazard if conditioned
379	 policies are ever introduced on those resource types (conditions
380	 would be silently mangled and destroyed on read-modify-write).
381	|===
382	
383	===== Convergence Analysis
384	
385	Based on these findings, the following convergence opportunities exist:
386	
387	**Safe to converge (zero new state space):**
388	
389	* **Always extract and pass etag intentionally**: Every API always returns etag.
390	  Extracting it explicitly and passing it to `setIamPolicy` converts accidental
391	  passthrough or unconditional overwrites to intentionally concurrency-safe
392	  writes.  The compose function already handles etag correctly when provided.
393	  This also protects against Google-internal concurrent policy modifications
394	  that the single-writer invariant does not cover.
395	
396	* **Transient retry on SET (429/5xx)**: Currently only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project}
397	  retries transient errors.  Adding bounded retry for 429 (rate limit) and 5xx
398	  (server error) is universally correct and cannot cause new failures.
399	
400	* **Uniform etag-mismatch handling**: Handle 409 ABORTED for all standard IAM
401	  mixin APIs, and 412 Precondition Failed for {gcs_service} only.  The current
402	  project code's 412 handler should become a 409 handler.
403	
404	* **Request policy version 3 everywhere**: Protects against silent condition
405	  destruction if conditioned policies are ever introduced on repo, SA, or
406	  bucket resources.  For APIs that use GET (where version is a query param),
407	  this is a query parameter addition; for POST APIs, it is a request body
408	  field.
409	
410	**Load-bearing differences (do not homogenize):**
411	
412	* **GET vs POST for getIamPolicy**: API-mandated per service proto.
413	  Cannot be unified.
414	
415	* **PUT vs POST for setIamPolicy**: {gcs_service} requires PUT.
416	  Cannot be unified.
417	
418	* **Body wrapping** (`{"policy":...}` vs raw): {gcs_service} requires raw.
419	  Cannot be unified.
420	
421	* **Project non-200 GET is a real error**: Projects always have IAM policies.
422	  Do not vanilla-init on non-200 for projects.
423	
424	**Require investigation before converging** (tracked in <<rbshr_horizon_roadmap>>)**:**
425	
426	* **Read-back verification universalization**
427	* **SA preflight universalization**
428	* **SA numeric ID for member references**
429	
430	===== Evidence
431	
432	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
433	|===
434	|Claim
435	|Source
436	
437	|Etag is a required field of google.iam.v1.Policy
438	|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/iam/v1/policy.proto[google.iam.v1.policy.proto] — `bytes etag = 3`
439	
440	|CRM setIamPolicy returns 409 on etag mismatch
441	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — "There were concurrent policy changes" with HTTP 409
442	
443	|{gcs_service} setIamPolicy returns 412 on etag mismatch
444	|https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/7853[Terraform #7853] — empirical 412 `conditionNotMet` with `location: "If-Match"`
445	
446	|Omitting etag allows unconditional overwrite, can destroy conditions
447	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/Policy[IAM Policy reference] — "If you omit this field, then IAM lets you overwrite a version 3 allow policy with a version 1 allow policy"
448	
449	|All resources are created with default allow policies
450	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — "All resources that accept allow policies are created with default allow policies"
451	
452	|{gar_registry} getIamPolicy uses HTTP GET
453	|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/devtools/artifactregistry/v1/service.proto[Artifact Registry service.proto] — `get: "/v1/{resource=...}:getIamPolicy"`
454	
455	|Secret Manager getIamPolicy uses HTTP GET
456	|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/cloud/secretmanager/v1/service.proto[Secret Manager service.proto] — `get: "/v1/{resource=...}:getIamPolicy"`
457	
458	|{gcs_service} uses GET/PUT (legacy REST, pre-gRPC transcoding)
459	|https://docs.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/getIamPolicy[buckets.getIamPolicy] — GET method; https://docs.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/setIamPolicy[buckets.setIamPolicy] — PUT method
460	
461	|SA propagation delay: typically < 2 minutes, can exceed 7 minutes
462	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/access-change-propagation[Access change propagation]; https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] — "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more"
463	
464	|Google recommends numeric ID for newly-created SAs
465	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] — "To identify a service account just after it is created, use its numeric ID rather than its email address"
466	
467	|400 "does not exist" is empirically stable across ecosystem
468	|https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1896218[Red Hat #1896218]; https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/8280[Terraform #8280]; https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-gcp/issues/1028[Pulumi #1028]
469	
470	|Terraform treats 400 as retryable in IAM contexts
471	|https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/8280[Terraform #8280] — 400 errors treated as concurrent-modification conflicts with retry logic
472	
473	|CRM returns 403 (not 404) for nonexistent projects
474	|https://docs.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/getIamPolicy[projects.getIamPolicy] — "Permission is denied if the policy or the resource do not exist"
475	
476	|IAM SA getIamPolicy returns 200 with empty policy for new SAs
477	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/getIamPolicy[serviceAccounts.getIamPolicy] — "returns an empty policy if the resource exists and does not have a policy set"
478	
479	|requestedPolicyVersion controls condition visibility
480	|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — version 1 response mangles conditions into `_withcond_` role suffixes
481	
482	|updateMask defaults to `bindings, etag`
483	|https://docs.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/setIamPolicy[projects.setIamPolicy] — "OPTIONAL: A FieldMask specifying which fields of the policy to modify"
484	
485	|Secret Manager supports regional secrets
486	|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/cloud/secretmanager/v1/service.proto[Secret Manager service.proto] — `additional_bindings` for `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*`
487	|===
488	
489	===== References
490	
491	* Research conducted: 2026-03-11
492	* Related: <<rbscip_trade_study>> — IAM propagation retry backoff profile
493	* Google API Improvement Proposals: https://google.aip.dev/136[AIP-136 Custom methods], https://google.aip.dev/127[AIP-127 HTTP and gRPC Transcoding]
494	* Upstream proto: https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/iam/v1/iam_policy.proto[google.iam.v1.IAMPolicy service definition]

version=1 does not downgrade a version=3 policy when the mask is defaulted —
the version field is simply ignored.

===== Empty-Policy / No-Policy Behavior

[cols="2,3,1",options="header"]
|===
|API
|getIamPolicy on new resource
|404 possible?

|CRM Projects ({rbtoe_iam_grant_project})
|200 + policy with bindings (project born with Owner grant)
|No (403 if project does not exist — deliberate enumeration prevention)

|IAM {giam_service_account_s} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_sa})
|200 + empty policy with etag
|No

|{gar_registry} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_repo})
|200 + empty policy with etag
|No

|Secret Manager
|200 + empty policy with etag
|No (404 = secret itself does not exist)

|{gcs_service} ({rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket})
|200 + policy with inherited bindings
|No
|===

**No API returns 404 for "no IAM policy."**
All resources that accept allow policies are created with default allow policies.
`getIamPolicy` returns 200 with an empty-ish policy (no bindings, but etag and
version present) for resources that have never had explicit IAM configuration.

===== Propagation Error Strings

The HTTP 400 errors containing `"does not exist"` and `"is not deleted"` that
the grant functions retry on (per <<rbscip_trade_study>> backoff profile) are
**empirically stable but not contractually guaranteed**.
Google does not document error message text as stable API surface.
The HTTP status code (400) is the stable signal; the message content is
diagnostic, not machine-parseable contract.

The two patterns represent different propagation directions:

* `"does not exist"` — forward propagation: a newly-created {giam_service_account}
  is not yet visible to the IAM policy service as a valid member.  The SA
  resource has been created (GET returns 200) but the policy service has not yet
  learned about it.

* `"is not deleted"` — backward propagation: a recently-deleted
  {giam_service_account} is still referenced in existing policy bindings.
  The SA resource has been deleted but the policy service still considers it
  a valid (or at least referenced) member.

Multiple independent ecosystem sources (Terraform provider, Red Hat OpenShift
installer, Pulumi) have depended on these strings for years without breakage.
The Terraform Google provider explicitly treats HTTP 400 as retryable in IAM
contexts (issue #8280), which constitutes strong ecosystem evidence that this
pattern is reliable enough for major infrastructure tools.
The practical risk of string instability is low but nonzero.

**SA email vs numeric ID propagation.**
Google's documentation recommends using a service account's numeric ID rather
than its email address to reference it immediately after creation: "To identify
a service account just after it is created, use its numeric ID rather than its
email address."  Recipe Bottle uses email addresses throughout, which is why
propagation retry is necessary.  Switching to numeric ID could reduce (but
likely not eliminate) propagation delays.

**SA keys.create write-path propagation.**
Observed 2026-03-12 during fresh depot onboarding: `serviceAccounts.keys.create`
POST returned HTTP 404 "Service account ... does not exist" immediately after
both `serviceAccounts.get` (GET by email) and `serviceAccounts.keys.list` (GET)
succeeded on the same SA email path.  This demonstrates a third propagation
class: SA read operations (GET by email, keys.list) propagate independently
from SA write operations (keys.create) on the same endpoint family.
The 404 response confirms the POST was rejected without side effects — no key
was created — making retry safe.  `gcloud iam service-accounts keys list`
confirmed zero USER_MANAGED keys after the 404 failure.
The race is intermittent: a subsequent director creation (dir1011) with the same
code path succeeded on first attempt.
Mitigation: retry keys.create POST on 404 up to `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX`
times with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC` delay between attempts.

===== Secret Manager Regional Endpoints

Secret Manager supports both global and regional secret endpoints:

* Global: `projects/*/secrets/*`
* Regional: `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*`

The service proto defines `additional_bindings` for regional secrets at both
`getIamPolicy` and `setIamPolicy`.  The current {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret} grants use global endpoints.  If Recipe Bottle ever uses regional
secrets, the IAM grant patterns would need to construct regional resource paths.
Noting for completeness.

===== Current Code vs Actual API Contract

This section catalogs discrepancies between the current implementation and the
verified API contracts.  These are not necessarily bugs that cause failures —
several work by accident or are protected by the single-writer operational
invariant — but they represent incorrect assumptions in the code.

[cols="1,2,3",options="header"]
|===
|Pattern
|Discrepancy
|Detail

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_project}
|412 handler targets wrong error code
|Code handles 412 as "etag mismatch" (line 199 of rbgi_IAM.sh).
 CRM actually returns 409 ABORTED.
 The 412 case never fires; etag mismatches hit the 409 path
 (`buc_die "HTTP 409 Conflict (fatal by invariant)"`), which
 produces the correct outcome (fatal) but with the wrong diagnostic.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
|404 handler for impossible response
|Code initializes vanilla policy on HTTP 404 (line 304).
 {gar_registry} `getIamPolicy` returns 200 with empty policy
 for existing repos, never 404.
 A 404 would mean the repository itself does not exist — vanilla init
 would mask this real error.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
|Resolved — uses GET per proto
|The shared `rbgi_add_repo_iam_role` function already used GET.
 The inline director AR grant path in `rbgg_Governor.sh` was the
 remaining POST site — observed failing with HTTP 404 on 2026-03-12
 (transcoding layer rejecting POST on GET-only endpoint).  Fixed to
 GET with `requestedPolicyVersion=3` query parameter.  The 404
 vanilla-init handler was also removed (404 on GET means repo does
 not exist — a real error, not a policy absence).

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}
|Skips available etag
|Passes empty string for etag (line 315), performing unconditional
 overwrite despite the API always returning etag.
 Safe under single-writer invariant but discards protection the API
 provides for free.  See "Accidental etag passthrough" above — the
 actual behavior may be passthrough rather than omission, but this
 is accidental, not intentional.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}
|Vanilla init for impossible response
|Code initializes vanilla policy on non-200 (line 411).
 IAM `getIamPolicy` returns 200 with empty policy for existing SAs.
 Non-200 means the SA does not exist or access denied — vanilla init
 masks a real error.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}
|Skips available etag
|Passes empty string for etag (line 419), performing unconditional
 overwrite.  Same single-writer safety, same accidental passthrough
 possibility, same missed intentional protection.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
|Etag described as conditional
|Code extracts etag with fallback to empty (line 507).
 {gcs_service} `getIamPolicy` always returns etag — the fallback
 should never trigger.  Minor: the RBS0 spec previously said "when
 present" but it is always present.

|Secret Manager ({rbtoe_iam_grant_secret})
|Resolved — now has propagation retry
|The three secret IAM grants (formerly inline in {rbtgo_depot_levy})
 are now extracted into {rbtoe_iam_grant_secret} with full propagation
 retry per <<rbscip_trade_study>> backoff profile, explicit etag,
 transient retry, and `requestedPolicyVersion=3`.

|{rbtoe_iam_grant_repo}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_sa}, {rbtoe_iam_grant_bucket}
|No requestedPolicyVersion in getIamPolicy
|Only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project} requests policy version 3.
 The other three do not, creating a latent hazard if conditioned
 policies are ever introduced on those resource types (conditions
 would be silently mangled and destroyed on read-modify-write).
|===

===== Convergence Analysis

Based on these findings, the following convergence opportunities exist:

**Safe to converge (zero new state space):**

* **Always extract and pass etag intentionally**: Every API always returns etag.
  Extracting it explicitly and passing it to `setIamPolicy` converts accidental
  passthrough or unconditional overwrites to intentionally concurrency-safe
  writes.  The compose function already handles etag correctly when provided.
  This also protects against Google-internal concurrent policy modifications
  that the single-writer invariant does not cover.

* **Transient retry on SET (429/5xx)**: Currently only {rbtoe_iam_grant_project}
  retries transient errors.  Adding bounded retry for 429 (rate limit) and 5xx
  (server error) is universally correct and cannot cause new failures.

* **Uniform etag-mismatch handling**: Handle 409 ABORTED for all standard IAM
  mixin APIs, and 412 Precondition Failed for {gcs_service} only.  The current
  project code's 412 handler should become a 409 handler.

* **Request policy version 3 everywhere**: Protects against silent condition
  destruction if conditioned policies are ever introduced on repo, SA, or
  bucket resources.  For APIs that use GET (where version is a query param),
  this is a query parameter addition; for POST APIs, it is a request body
  field.

**Load-bearing differences (do not homogenize):**

* **GET vs POST for getIamPolicy**: API-mandated per service proto.
  Cannot be unified.

* **PUT vs POST for setIamPolicy**: {gcs_service} requires PUT.
  Cannot be unified.

* **Body wrapping** (`{"policy":...}` vs raw): {gcs_service} requires raw.
  Cannot be unified.

* **Project non-200 GET is a real error**: Projects always have IAM policies.
  Do not vanilla-init on non-200 for projects.

**Require investigation before converging** (tracked in <<rbshr_horizon_roadmap>>)**:**

* **Read-back verification universalization**
* **SA preflight universalization**
* **SA numeric ID for member references**

===== Evidence

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Claim
|Source

|Etag is a required field of google.iam.v1.Policy
|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/iam/v1/policy.proto[google.iam.v1.policy.proto] — `bytes etag = 3`

|CRM setIamPolicy returns 409 on etag mismatch
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — "There were concurrent policy changes" with HTTP 409

|{gcs_service} setIamPolicy returns 412 on etag mismatch
|https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/7853[Terraform #7853] — empirical 412 `conditionNotMet` with `location: "If-Match"`

|Omitting etag allows unconditional overwrite, can destroy conditions
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/Policy[IAM Policy reference] — "If you omit this field, then IAM lets you overwrite a version 3 allow policy with a version 1 allow policy"

|All resources are created with default allow policies
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — "All resources that accept allow policies are created with default allow policies"

|{gar_registry} getIamPolicy uses HTTP GET
|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/devtools/artifactregistry/v1/service.proto[Artifact Registry service.proto] — `get: "/v1/{resource=...}:getIamPolicy"`

|Secret Manager getIamPolicy uses HTTP GET
|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/cloud/secretmanager/v1/service.proto[Secret Manager service.proto] — `get: "/v1/{resource=...}:getIamPolicy"`

|{gcs_service} uses GET/PUT (legacy REST, pre-gRPC transcoding)
|https://docs.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/getIamPolicy[buckets.getIamPolicy] — GET method; https://docs.google.com/storage/docs/json_api/v1/buckets/setIamPolicy[buckets.setIamPolicy] — PUT method

|SA propagation delay: typically < 2 minutes, can exceed 7 minutes
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/access-change-propagation[Access change propagation]; https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] — "you might need to wait 60 seconds or more"

|Google recommends numeric ID for newly-created SAs
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/service-accounts-create[Creating service accounts] — "To identify a service account just after it is created, use its numeric ID rather than its email address"

|400 "does not exist" is empirically stable across ecosystem
|https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1896218[Red Hat #1896218]; https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/8280[Terraform #8280]; https://github.com/pulumi/pulumi-gcp/issues/1028[Pulumi #1028]

|Terraform treats 400 as retryable in IAM contexts
|https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-provider-google/issues/8280[Terraform #8280] — 400 errors treated as concurrent-modification conflicts with retry logic

|CRM returns 403 (not 404) for nonexistent projects
|https://docs.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/getIamPolicy[projects.getIamPolicy] — "Permission is denied if the policy or the resource do not exist"

|IAM SA getIamPolicy returns 200 with empty policy for new SAs
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.serviceAccounts/getIamPolicy[serviceAccounts.getIamPolicy] — "returns an empty policy if the resource exists and does not have a policy set"

|requestedPolicyVersion controls condition visibility
|https://docs.google.com/iam/docs/allow-policies[Understanding allow policies] — version 1 response mangles conditions into `_withcond_` role suffixes

|updateMask defaults to `bindings, etag`
|https://docs.google.com/resource-manager/reference/rest/v3/projects/setIamPolicy[projects.setIamPolicy] — "OPTIONAL: A FieldMask specifying which fields of the policy to modify"

|Secret Manager supports regional secrets
|https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/cloud/secretmanager/v1/service.proto[Secret Manager service.proto] — `additional_bindings` for `projects/*/locations/*/secrets/*`
|===

===== References

* Research conducted: 2026-03-11
* Related: <<rbscip_trade_study>> — IAM propagation retry backoff profile
* Google API Improvement Proposals: https://google.aip.dev/136[AIP-136 Custom methods], https://google.aip.dev/127[AIP-127 HTTP and gRPC Transcoding]
* Upstream proto: https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/iam/v1/iam_policy.proto[google.iam.v1.IAMPolicy service definition]

  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either

  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
  # cadence below.
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15

  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15

  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420

content.text: Now generalize the predicate. I'll keep the (code, body-glob)-pairs interface specified in the docket — empty glob = match-any-body short-circuits without loading the error message.

input.old_string:
# Check if an HTTP response is a 400 with a transient IAM propagation error.
# Two known patterns:
#   - "does not exist": newly-created SA not yet visible to policy service
#   - "is not deleted": recently-deleted SA still referenced in policy bindings
# Returns 0 (true) if retryable propagation error, 1 otherwise.
zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate() {
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_code="${2}"

  test "${z_code}" = "400" || return 1

  local z_err_msg=""
  z_err_msg=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_infix}") || z_err_msg=""

  case "${z_err_msg}" in
    *"does not exist"*) return 0 ;;
    *"is not deleted"*) return 0 ;;
    *)                  return 1 ;;
  esac
}
input.new_string:
# Classify an HTTP response against a caller-supplied tolerance list.
# Each tolerance is a (code, body-glob) pair passed as two positional args
# after the infix and code. An empty body-glob matches any response body
# for that code — short-circuits without loading the error message.
#
# Three propagation classes are encoded as tolerance pairs:
#   (400, "*does not exist*") — forward member-visibility lag
#   (400, "*is not deleted*") — backward member-visibility lag
#   (403, "")                 — caller-recently-empowered (resource-scope cache lag)
#
# Project-scope sites declare only the two 400 patterns; resource-scope sites
# (AR repo, SA, bucket, secret) add the 403 pair. Time-bound on 403 is the
# discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial waits
# the budget and fails cleanly.
#
# Returns 0 (true) if response matches any tolerance pair, 1 otherwise.
zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate() {
  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_code="${2}"
  shift 2

  local z_err_msg=""
  local z_err_loaded=0
  local z_tol_code=""
  local z_tol_glob=""

  while test "$#" -ge 2; do
    z_tol_code="${1}"
    z_tol_glob="${2}"
    shift 2

    test "${z_code}" = "${z_tol_code}" || continue

    test -n "${z_tol_glob}" || return 0

    if test "${z_err_loaded}" = "0"; then
      z_err_msg=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_infix}") || z_err_msg=""
      z_err_loaded=1
    fi

    # shellcheck disable=SC2254  # intentional glob match against z_tol_glob
    case "${z_err_msg}" in
      ${z_tol_glob}) return 0 ;;
    esac
  done

  return 1
}

content.text: Now I'll update each grant site in `rbgi_IAM.sh`. Project-scope first (no 403 tolerance):

input.old_string:
buc_log_args "${z_label}: add ${z_member} to ${z_role}"

  # Propagation retry: steps 1-3 may fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
  # when a newly-created SA hasn't propagated to the IAM policy service.
  # Exponential backoff: 3s initial, 2x multiplier, 20s cap, 420s deadline.
  local z_prop_delay=3
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local z_prop_deadline=420
  local z_prop_attempt=0
  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))

    buc_log_args "1) GET policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    buc_log_args "GET_POLICY_URL_DEBUG z_resource:${z_resource} z_get_url:${z_get_url}"
    local z_get_body="${ZRBGI_PREFIX}${z_parent_infix}_get_body.json"
    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    printf '%s\n' '{"options":{"requestedPolicyVersion":3}}' > "${z_get_body}"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_body}"

    local z_get_code=""
    z_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_get_code}"; then
      buc_log_args "${z_label}: getIamPolicy returned 400 'does not exist' (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
      continue
    fi
input.new_string:
buc_log_args "${z_label}: add ${z_member} to ${z_role}"

  # Propagation retry — project-scope tolerance: forward/backward
  # member-visibility (400 patterns). Class C (403 resource-scope cache lag)
  # does not apply at project scope.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0

# Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_code}"; then
        buc_log_args "${z_label}: setIamPolicy returned 400 'does not exist' (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        409)                 buc_die "${z_label}: HTTP 409 ABORTED (etag mismatch — concurrent policy change)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbgu_http_require_ok "${z_label} (set policy)" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "${z_label}: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s waiting for member visibility"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "${z_label}: propagation retry loop exited without success"

content.text: Now the SA grant — also resource-scope.

input.old_string:
local -r z_sa_resource="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}"

  # Propagation retry: get-modify-set may fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
  # when a newly-created SA hasn't propagated to the IAM policy service.
  # Exponential backoff: 3s initial, 2x multiplier, 20s cap, 420s deadline.
  local z_prop_delay=3
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local z_prop_deadline=420
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET SA IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:getIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
      "${z_get_infix}" "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}"; then
      buc_log_args "SA getIamPolicy returned 400 'does not exist' (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
      continue
    fi
input.new_string:
local -r z_sa_resource="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}/projects/-/serviceAccounts/${z_target_encoded}"

  # Propagation retry — SA is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_ROLE}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET SA IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_sa_resource}:getIamPolicy" "${z_token}" \
      "${z_get_infix}" "${ZRBGI_VERSION3_BODY}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from SA getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "SA getIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "SA IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

content.text: Bucket grant — also resource-scope.

input.old_string:
local -r z_iam_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/iam"

  # Propagation retry: get-modify-set may fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
  # when a newly-created SA hasn't propagated to the IAM policy service.
  # Exponential backoff: 3s initial, 2x multiplier, 20s cap, 420s deadline.
  local z_prop_delay=3
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local z_prop_deadline=420
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_BUCKET_IAM}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET bucket IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_iam_url}?optionsRequestedPolicyVersion=3" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from bucket getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}"; then
      buc_log_args "Bucket getIamPolicy returned 400 'does not exist' (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "Bucket IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
      continue
    fi
input.new_string:
local -r z_iam_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/iam"

  # Propagation retry — bucket is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache.
  local -ra z_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_prop_elapsed=0
  local z_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${ZRBGI_INFIX_BUCKET_IAM}-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET bucket IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_iam_url}?optionsRequestedPolicyVersion=3" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from bucket getIamPolicy"

    # Check for propagation error on GET
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_get_infix}" "${z_code}" "${z_tolerance[@]}"; then
      buc_log_args "Bucket getIamPolicy returned ${z_code} (propagation delay)"
      test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "Bucket IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
      sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
      z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

input.old_string:
# Check for propagation error on SET — break inner loop to retry outer
      if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_set_infix}" "${z_set_code}"; then
        buc_log_args "Bucket setIamPolicy returned 400 'does not exist' (propagation delay)"
        break
      fi

      case "${z_set_code}" in
        200)                 z_set_succeeded=1; break ;;
        412)                 buc_die "Bucket IAM: HTTP 412 Precondition Failed (etag mismatch)" ;;
        409)                 buc_die "Bucket IAM: HTTP 409 ABORTED (defensive — unexpected for Storage)" ;;
        429|500|502|503|504) buc_log_args "Transient ${z_set_code} at ${z_set_elapsed}s; retry" ;;
        *)                   rbgu_http_require_ok "Set bucket IAM policy" "${z_set_infix}" "" ;;
      esac

      test "${z_set_elapsed}" -lt "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" || buc_die "Bucket IAM: timeout setting policy"
      sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
      z_set_elapsed=$((z_set_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
    done

    # If setIamPolicy succeeded, break outer propagation loop
    test "${z_set_succeeded}" != "1" || { z_prop_succeeded=1; break; }

    # setIamPolicy hit propagation error — retry outer loop with fresh getIamPolicy
    test "${z_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_prop_deadline}" \
      || buc_die "Bucket IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_prop_elapsed}s"
    buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"
    sleep "${z_prop_delay}"
    z_prop_elapsed=$((z_prop_elapsed + z_prop_delay))
    z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
    test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
  done

  test "${z_prop_succeeded}" = "1" || buc_die "Bucket IAM: propagation retry loop exited without success"

input.old_string:
local -r z_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER}${RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1}/${z_secret_resource_path}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER}${RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1}/${z_secret_resource_path}:setIamPolicy"

  local z_prop_attempt=0

  local z_prop_succeeded=0

  while :; do
    z_prop_attempt=$((z_prop_attempt + 1))
    local z_get_infix="${z_parent_infix}-get-${z_prop_elapsed}s"

    buc_log_args "1) GET secret IAM policy (v3) [attempt ${z_prop_attempt}]"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_get_infix}"

    local z_get_code
    z_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_get_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from secret getIamPolicy"

      buc_log_args "Retry ${z_prop_attempt} at ${z_prop_elapsed}s (next delay ${z_prop_delay}s)"

      z_prop_delay=$((z_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_prop_delay=20
      continue
    fi

input.old_string:
buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  local z_gar_prop_delay=3
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=420
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""
  local z_gar_get_err=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry: newly-created Director SA may not be visible yet
    if test "${z_gar_get_code}" = "400"; then
      z_gar_get_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_err=""
      case "${z_gar_get_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET
    if test "${z_gar_set_code}" = "400"; then
      local z_gar_set_err=""
      z_gar_set_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || z_gar_set_err=""
      case "${z_gar_set_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache (the
  # governor's roles/owner grant may not yet have reached the AR cache).
  local -ra z_gar_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry on GET — covers newly-empowered governor (403) and
    # newly-created Director SA member-visibility lag (400 patterns).
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_get_infix}" "${z_gar_get_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_get_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET — same tolerance list as GET.
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_set_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET
    if test "${z_gar_set_code}" = "400"; then
      local z_gar_set_err=""
      z_gar_set_err=$(rbgu_error_message_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || z_gar_set_err=""
      case "${z_gar_set_err}" in
        *"does not exist"*|*"is not deleted"*)
          test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
            || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
          buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy propagation delay (attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
          sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
          z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
          z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
          test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le 20 || z_gar_prop_delay=20
          continue
          ;;
      esac
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"

  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache (the
  # governor's roles/owner grant may not yet have reached the AR cache).
  local -ra z_gar_tolerance=(
    "400" "*does not exist*"
    "400" "*is not deleted*"
    "403" ""
  )
  local z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
  local z_gar_get_infix=""
  local z_gar_set_infix=""
  local z_gar_get_code=""

  while :; do
    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"

    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
    z_gar_get_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""

    # Propagation retry on GET — covers newly-empowered governor (403) and
    # newly-created Director SA member-visibility lag (400 patterns).
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_get_infix}" "${z_gar_get_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_get_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"

    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
    local z_gar_partial
    z_gar_partial=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgu_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_complete
    z_gar_complete=$(rbgu_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"

    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"

    local z_gar_set_code
    z_gar_set_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"

    # Propagation retry on SET — same tolerance list as GET.
    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
      buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_set_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
      continue
    fi

    rbgu_http_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
    break
  done

content.text: Continuing Tier A specs. Starting with RBSCIP — generalize the propagation discussion to three classes and reference constants.

input.old_string:
IAM propagation delays for Cloud Build v2 connection creation are handled by
connection-creation retry with exponential backoff, not by an impersonation
readiness gate or fixed sleep delays.

* **SA binding propagation**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy` calls that fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist"
  (newly-created service account not yet visible to the IAM policy service) are retried with exponential backoff.
* **Secret access readiness**: The CB v2 connection creation LRO itself serves as the propagation test.
  If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (IAM grant not propagated), the LRO fails.
  The caller retries with exponential backoff, deleting the zombie connection resource between attempts.
* **Secret resource propagation**: `getIamPolicy` on a newly created secret may return HTTP 404 while
  the Secret Manager resource propagates across Google's control plane.  The API documents that
  `getIamPolicy` returns an empty policy (HTTP 200) when no policy has been set — so 404 indicates
  the resource itself is not yet visible, not that the policy is absent.  After creating secrets,
  `rbgu_poll_until_ok` polls each secret's `getIamPolicy` endpoint until 200 before proceeding to
  grant IAM bindings.
input.new_string:
IAM propagation delays for Cloud Build v2 connection creation are handled by
connection-creation retry with exponential backoff, not by an impersonation
readiness gate or fixed sleep delays.

Three propagation classes are recognized by the IAM grant harness in
`zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate`, all sharing the same exponential-backoff
budget defined by the `RBGC_PROPAGATION_*` constants:

* **Class A — forward member-visibility**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy`
  calls fail with HTTP 400 "does not exist" when a newly-created service
  account is not yet visible to the IAM policy service as a valid member.
* **Class B — backward member-visibility**: `setIamPolicy` and `getIamPolicy`
  calls fail with HTTP 400 "is not deleted" when a recently-deleted service
  account is still referenced in existing policy bindings.
* **Class C — caller-recently-empowered**: `getIamPolicy` / `setIamPolicy`
  on a resource-scope target (AR repo, SA, bucket, secret) returns HTTP 403
  because the caller's recently-granted role (e.g. fresh `roles/owner` on a
  newly-levied depot) has not yet reached the resource-scope IAM cache,
  even though it has reached project- and SA-scope caches.

Class C is time-bounded only — no body fingerprinting. The bound itself is
the discriminator: real propagation succeeds within budget, real denial
waits the budget and fails cleanly. The same model is already proven in
{rbtgo_access_jwt_probe}. Resource-scope grant sites declare 403 tolerance
with an empty body-glob; project-scope sites do not.

* **Secret access readiness**: The CB v2 connection creation LRO itself serves as the propagation test.
  If the Cloud Build Service Agent cannot yet access the secrets (IAM grant not propagated), the LRO fails.
  The caller retries with exponential backoff, deleting the zombie connection resource between attempts.
* **Secret resource propagation**: `getIamPolicy` on a newly created secret may return HTTP 404 while
  the Secret Manager resource propagates across Google's control plane.  The API documents that
  `getIamPolicy` returns an empty policy (HTTP 200) when no policy has been set — so 404 indicates
  the resource itself is not yet visible, not that the policy is absent.  After creating secrets,
  `rbgu_poll_until_ok` polls each secret's `getIamPolicy` endpoint until 200 before proceeding to
  grant IAM bindings.
